title: HeaderButton
text: HeaderButton The HeaderButton is a logical widget for styling a button to look like a column heading. Figure 1. Header buttons For more information, see Specifying a Widget for a Field or Entity . Table 1. Widget Summary Logical widget name: CommandButton Maps to physical widget: ucmdbutton Default definition: HeaderButton=ucmdbutton(tooltip=on;font=buttonfont;representation=header) Supported triggers and properties: See ucmdbutton Use for: Static or control fields (as defined by the Is Static and Is Control properties) of type String. Grid and MSAA Support Supported in Grid? Yes, but not as column header. Some properties may not be applicable. For more information, see egrid (Grid) and Widgets and Properties Supported in Grid Widget . Supports MSAA? No. Description The HeaderButton is typically used as a column heading when styling an entity as a table, but without using the Grid widget. The HeaderButton follows Windows conventions. The text displayed on the button comes from the field value. Related Links Defining Command Buttons Command buttons enable the user to perform specific actions. They should only be used in form components, not in reports. KeyPadEnterAccess Specifies whether the keypad Enter key is routed through the keyboard translation table for command buttons.
